svg.title: DNS Records for Email — Complete Map
svg.desc: Complete reference of all DNS records that affect email deliverability, organized by function: authentication records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, MX), infrastructure records (PTR/reverse DNS), and advanced security and brand records (MTA-STS, TLS-RPT, BIMI). Each record shows the DNS record type, the hostname where it lives, what it does, an example value, and whether it is required, recommended, or optional for bulk email senders. SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX are required by Google, Microsoft, and Yahoo. PTR is required by Google for all sending IPs. MTA-STS and TLS-RPT are recommended for TLS enforcement and monitoring. BIMI is optional for brand logo display in supporting inboxes. Includes a record location quick reference showing exactly what to enter in the DNS host field for each record, and a common mistakes panel covering the most frequent DNS configuration errors.

cc:Work.dc:description: Complete DNS record reference for email deliverability covering authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, MX), infrastructure (PTR), and advanced records (MTA-STS, TLS-RPT, BIMI) with requirement tiers, example values, and hostname locations.

svg.text: COMPLETE MAP — EVERY RECORD THAT AFFECTS DELIVERABILITY

g.aria-label: Legend showing DNS record types and requirement tiers
g.data-description: Color-coded badges for DNS record types (TXT, MX, PTR, CNAME) and requirement tiers (Required, Recommended, Optional)
g.text: Receiving providers query your DNS on every delivery attempt

g.data-description: Required DNS records for email authentication including SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX — all four required by Google, Microsoft, and Yahoo for bulk senders
g.text: Required by Google, Microsoft, and Yahoo for bulk senders
Specifies which servers are authorized to send for your domain
Providers check Return-Path domain against SPF during delivery
One SPF record per domain. Multiple records = permerror.
Cryptographic signature proving message wasn't altered in transit
Provider retrieves your public key from DNS to verify signature
v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEB... (or CNAME to ESP)
Selector chosen by you/ESP. Most ESPs use CNAME for key rotation.
Tells providers what to do when SPF/DKIM fail or don't align
Policy: none (monitor) → quarantine → reject. Reports via rua tag.
Start p=none, escalate to reject. Without rua, you enforce blind.
Specifies which servers accept incoming mail for your domain
Missing MX signals illegitimate domain — affects sender reputation
Priority numbers (10, 20) set failover order. Lower = tried first.

g.data-description: PTR reverse DNS record required for IP reputation — maps sending IP back to a hostname that must forward-confirm
g.text: Maps your sending IP back to a hostname — provider checks it forward-confirms (PTR → A → same IP)
Missing or mismatched PTR is a strong spam signal. Google explicitly requires valid PTR for all sending IPs.
PTR should resolve to your hostname (e.g., mail.yourdomain.com)
Contact your hosting provider to set. Verify it forward-confirms.
PTR points to ESP hostname (e.g., o1.ptr7890.sendgrid.net)
This is expected. Your sender identity comes from DKIM, not PTR.

g.aria-label: Advanced Security and Brand DNS Records
g.data-description: MTA-STS for TLS enforcement, TLS-RPT for TLS failure reporting, and BIMI for brand logo display — recommended or optional for enhanced security and brand recognition
g.text: Enforces strict TLS for inbound email
Inbound only — not a factor in outbound sender evaluation
Also requires policy file hosted at
Where to send TLS delivery failure reports
JSON reports delivered daily. Same concept
as DMARC rua but for TLS failures.
Displays your brand logo in supporting inboxes
Increases recipient engagement signals that feed sender reputation
Gmail requires VMC (~$1,500/yr). Yahoo/Apple do not.

g.data-description: Compact table showing the exact DNS host field entry, record type, and purpose for each of the eight email DNS records
g.text: RECORD LOCATION QUICK REFERENCE — WHAT TO ENTER IN YOUR DNS PANEL
(set by hosting/IP provider, not your DNS panel)

g.data-description: Four most frequent DNS configuration errors for email: multiple SPF records, DKIM selector mismatch, DMARC without reporting, and PTR not set or not forward-confirming
g.text: Two v=spf1 records on one domain → permerror.
Merge into one record using include:
Key at wrong selector hostname. Verify selector
matches what your ESP/MTA signs with.
Monitoring policy with no monitoring — you enforce
nothing and learn nothing. Always include rua.
PTR not set or doesn't forward-confirm
Not in your DNS panel — contact your hosting or IP
provider (or ESP if they own the sending IPs).

g.data-description: Primary sources including IETF RFCs for each protocol, Google sender guidelines, and BIMI Working Group specifications
g.text: RFC 7208 (SPF) · RFC 6376 (DKIM) · RFC 7489 (DMARC) · RFC 5321 (MX) · RFC 1035 (PTR) · RFC 8461 (MTA-STS) · RFC 8460 (TLS-RPT)
